hypothesis: mynn_pbl.py qv solve is missing the MYNN-EDMF mass-flux scalar transport (s_awqv); this causes the d03 daytime near-surface qv dry bias / QFX & LH deficit.

config_resolved.consequence: ONLY s_awqv matters: sub_sqv=det_sqv=0 (env_subs=.false.), sd_*=0 (no downdraft), no momentum MF.

verification_vs_WRF_oracle.method: JAX dmp_mf_columns vs pristine WRF DMP_mf (linked module_bl_mynnedmf.o), single column, single step
verification_vs_WRF_oracle.note: residual is fp32-WRF vs fp64-JAX + plume tanh/condensation nonlinearity

confirmation.structural_fact: s_awqv(kts)=0 always -> MF never directly sources lowest-level qv in one step; it reshapes the PBL above
confirmation.verdict: PARTIAL CONFIRMATION: MF ventilates moisture UP; with responsive surface flux it RAISES evaporation +7-11% (matches equiv-T2 QFX~60%/LH-8.7 deficit). But MF DRIES the near-surface in a column experiment, so missing MF is NOT the sole/dominant cause of the -1.74e-3 near-surface dry bias -- that is coupled with the land-side canopy vapor source (EAH 1467 vs 1655 Pa).

landed.tests: tests/test_mynn_edmf_oracle.py (2 pass); 11 existing M5 MYNN tests unchanged

remaining: Wire edmf=True into the operational coupler (physics_couplers.py mynn_adapter) -- OTHER LANE's file; manager to enable + GPU remeasure.
Plume condensation (qc>0 saturated PBL / stratocu) untested -- this column was dry (qc=0); s_awqc verified=0 here only.
Skin-temperature (ts) for the exact WRF superadiabatic guard not carried in the standalone column (buoyancy-flux fallback used; equivalent for daytime land).
Downdraft/subsidence/detrainment intentionally omitted (zero in this config); revisit if config changes.
